message:
Last winter, I got SO confident about a big storm forecast that I decided NOT to prep because, you know, 'we've handled worse.' Narrator voice: *they had not handled worse.* By hour three of the power out, I was eating cold chili straight from the can with a camping spoon while my family huddled under every blanket we owned. The comedy was real—and the lesson even more so.

Turns out, being ready isn't about living in fear. It's just... being ready. A little water, a battery-powered radio, some snacks that don't need cooking—nothing fancy or complicated. It's more like a quiet backup plan than a doomsday bunker.

Has a weather warning ever caught you off guard? What did you wish you'd had on hand?
